title: Calling the GitHub API
subtitle: Explore a production API — no key needed for public data

sections.content:
GitHub's REST API is one of the best APIs to learn with:
- **Free** for public data — no key needed
- **Extensively documented** at `docs.github.com/en/rest`
- **Real data** — your actual repos, commits, and profile
- **Standard patterns** — everything you learn here applies to most REST APIs

The base URL is `https://api.github.com`. All endpoints go after it.
keyPoints: No API key needed for public data (up to 60 requests/hour per IP)
Response data includes far more fields than you'd typically use — just pick what you need

sections.instruction: Fetch your own GitHub profile (or any public GitHub user). Replace `torvalds` with any GitHub username.
sections.expectedOutput: A JSON object with login, id, name, public_repos, followers, following, created_at, and many more fields.

sections.instruction: List the public repositories for that user.
sections.expectedOutput: An array of repository objects. Each has name, description, language, stargazers_count, forks_count, updated_at, and more.

sections.instruction: Add query parameters to filter the results — get the 3 most recently updated repos, sorted by update time.
sections.expectedOutput: An array of 3 repository objects, sorted with the most recently updated first.

sections.instruction: Now try it with YOUR GitHub username. Replace `YOUR_USERNAME` below.
sections.expectedOutput: Your 5 most recently updated repositories as JSON.

troubleshooting.solution: Your GitHub account may have no public repos yet, or they're all private. That's fine — the API call itself worked correctly.
Check that you typed your GitHub username correctly (case-insensitive, but must match the username in your GitHub URL).

sections.question: What is the base URL for the GitHub API?
sections.explanation: The GitHub REST API is available at https://api.github.com. All endpoints are paths appended to this base URL, such as /users/torvalds or /repos/facebook/react.

sections.prompt: To list a user's repos from the GitHub API, use the path `/users/USERNAME/_____`.

completionMessage: You just queried a production API used by millions of developers every day. The pattern — base URL + path + query params — works for every REST API on the internet.
